title: Parity closure and runtime utilities
summary: Forge Method now ships parity closure utility surfaces as opt-in Forge contracts, with dedicated routing, facilitation packs, compact templates, scripts, and replay coverage.
highlights: Dedicated routes now cover isolated eval runner, hook/event plan, and reusable API/browser utility requests.
Runtime utility guidance stays opt-in and disabled by default, avoiding hidden startup overhead.
Compact artifact templates capture trust boundaries, side effects, commands, cleanup, evidence, and waiver decisions.
Replay coverage now protects these utility routes as part of the broader guidance fixture suite.
Release/version skepticism now routes to release-readiness so publication claims are checked against version metadata and install evidence.
